overview.tagline: Where Excellent Schools Meet Natural Beauty
overview.description: Tenafly is an affluent residential borough in Bergen County, New Jersey, celebrated for its outstanding public schools, diverse and welcoming community, and proximity to the Palisades Interstate Park. Situated along the western slope of the New Jersey Palisades, the borough offers a unique blend of suburban tranquility and convenient access to New York City. Tenafly's vibrant downtown along Washington Street features local shops, restaurants, and a strong sense of community that draws families from around the world.
highlights: Top-ranked public school district consistently rated among the best in New Jersey
Adjacent to Palisades Interstate Park with miles of hiking trails
Diverse, multicultural community with significant Korean-American population
Easy NYC commute via NJ Transit bus

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County conducts periodic reassessments to ensure equitable valuation.
realEstate.recentTrends: Tenafly's housing market has been robust with strong demand driven by its school reputation and NYC accessibility. Bidding wars are common for well-maintained homes in prime locations. The east hill area commands the highest prices.
neighborhoodNotes: East Hill is the most prestigious area with larger homes and panoramic views toward the Palisades
The downtown/Washington Street area offers walkability and convenience
West Tenafly near the Cresskill border has a mix of colonials and split-levels
The area near Palisades Interstate Park is prized for nature access
North Tenafly near Alpine border features some of the most expensive properties

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly for residential properties by a contracted hauler.
details: Collection days are typically Monday/Thursday or Tuesday/Friday depending on location
Trash must be in sealed containers or bags and placed at the curb by 6:00 AM
Maximum of 6 containers or 10 bags per collection
Bulk item pickup is available by appointment
Holiday schedules are posted on the borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Weekly curbside recycling collection for all residential properties, coordinated through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Single-stream recycling collected once per week
Accepted: paper, cardboard, glass, plastics #1-#7, metal cans
Do not include plastic bags, styrofoam, or food waste
Yard waste collected separately in spring, summer, and fall
Recycling drop-off also available at the DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water. Sewer service is managed by the borough and treated by the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills are issued quarterly
Sewer charges are included in the tax billing
Annual water quality reports are available online
Contact Veolia for water service issues or new connections
Backflow prevention devices may be required for certain properties

permits.title: Building & Zoning Permits
permits.description: The Building Department oversees all construction and zoning permit applications. Permits are required for most exterior and interior modifications.
details: Permits required for construction, additions, renovations, and demolition
Zoning applications for variances heard by the Zoning Board of Adjustment
Tree removal permits required for trees over a certain caliper
Historic district considerations may apply in certain areas
Applications available at Borough Hall or on the borough website

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Tenafly has metered and permit parking in the downtown area to manage parking for commuters and shoppers.
details: Metered parking available on Washington Street and surrounding streets
Commuter parking permits available for NJ Transit bus riders
Residential permit parking in areas near the downtown core
Overnight parking restrictions on most borough streets
Parking enforcement is active in the downtown area

additionalServices.title: Leaf Collection
additionalServices.description: Seasonal leaf collection for all residential properties during fall months.
details: Loose leaf collection runs from mid-October through mid-December
Leaves should be raked to the curb, not into the street
Do not mix leaves with branches or other materials
Multiple passes through each neighborhood during the season

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Tenafly Recreation Department offers year-round programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues, swim programs, and summer camps
Adult fitness, tennis, and enrichment programs
Senior citizen programs and trips
Pool memberships at the Tenafly Swim Club (municipal)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Tenafly enforces noise regulations to preserve the residential character of the borough with specific quiet hours.
keyRules: Quiet hours: 10:00 PM to 8:00 AM on weekdays, 10:00 PM to 9:00 AM on weekends
Construction limited to 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM weekdays, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM Saturdays
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Gas-powered leaf blowers prohibited during certain months (seasonal restrictions)
Amplified music and sound systems must comply with decibel limits

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain structures and grounds in compliance with borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and not exceed 10 inches in height
Properties must be kept free of debris, junk, and abandoned vehicles
Exterior structures must be maintained in good repair and appearance
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within a reasonable time after a storm
Swimming pools must have proper fencing and safety barriers
propertyMaintenance.source: Borough of Tenafly Property Maintenance Code

pets.title: Pet & Animal Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs and cats must be licensed and vaccinated. Leash laws are strictly enforced.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be on a leash at all times when off the owner's property
Pet waste must be immediately cleaned up on public and private property
No more than 3 dogs per household
Excessive barking complaints are investigated by Animal Control
Cats should be vaccinated against rabies
pets.source: Borough of Tenafly Code, Animals Chapter

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated throughout the borough with restrictions on overnight parking and specific zones in the downtown area.
keyRules: No overnight street parking between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Metered parking enforced in the downtown business district
Commuter parking by permit only in designated lots
No commercial vehicle parking on residential streets
Snow emergency parking bans may be declared during storms

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with housing codes and landlords are subject to registration requirements.
keyRules: Rental units must meet all housing and building code standards
Landlords may need to register rental properties with the borough
Certificate of Occupancy or Habitability inspection at change of tenancy
Multi-family housing limited to specifically zoned areas
Short-term rental regulations may apply

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Tenafly has strict tree preservation ordinances to protect the borough's tree canopy.
keyRules: A permit is required to remove any tree over 6 inches in diameter at breast height
Replacement planting may be required when trees are removed
Protected species include oaks, maples, and other native hardwoods
Tree damage during construction is subject to penalties
The Shade Tree Commission reviews applications and advises on tree matters
other.source: Borough of Tenafly Code, Trees Chapter

other.title: Fence and Wall Regulations
other.summary: Fences and walls are regulated for height, location, and appearance.
keyRules: Front yard fences limited to 4 feet in height
Side and rear yard fences limited to 6 feet
Permits required for all new fences
Deer fencing may be permitted up to 8 feet with approval
The finished side of the fence must face the neighboring property

publicSchools.name: Maugham School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the central/north area of the borough

publicSchools.name: Malcolm S. Mackay School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the eastern section of the borough

publicSchools.name: Stillman School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the western section of Tenafly

publicSchools.name: Smith School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the southern area of the borough

publicSchools.name: Tenafly Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated middle school with strong STEM and arts programs

publicSchools.name: Tenafly High School
publicSchools.notes: Consistently ranked among the top public high schools in New Jersey; known for academic excellence, competitive math team, and robust AP course offerings

privateSchools.name: Yavneh Academy
privateSchools.notes: Modern Orthodox Jewish day school offering dual curriculum of Judaic and general studies

privateSchools.name: The Elisabeth Morrow School
privateSchools.notes: Independent school located in nearby Englewood, popular with Tenafly families

whyMoveHere: Outstanding public schools consistently ranked in the top tier statewide
Diverse, multicultural community that is welcoming to families from all backgrounds
Access to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking, biking, and nature
Charming downtown on Washington Street with local shops and restaurants
Safe, family-oriented neighborhoods with excellent property values

commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes 166 and 167 provide direct service from Tenafly to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. Commuter parking is available in the downtown area. Driving to the George Washington Bridge takes approximately 10-15 minutes. Many residents also drive to the Englewood Cliffs ferry or nearby train stations.

costOfLiving.summary: Tenafly is one of the more expensive communities in Bergen County, with high home prices and property taxes reflecting the quality of schools and community amenities.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: About 40% above the New Jersey average

pros: Top-ranked public schools attract families globally
Strong sense of community and cultural diversity
Beautiful natural setting near the Palisades
Low crime rate and safe neighborhoods
cons: Very high property taxes (among the highest in the state)
High home prices limit entry for many buyers
No train station; bus commute dependent

restaurants.name: Amici Ristorante
restaurants.description: Popular Italian restaurant offering traditional pasta, seafood, and meat dishes in a warm, family-friendly atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Sushi Seki
restaurants.description: High-quality Japanese sushi restaurant known for its fresh fish and omakase offerings.

restaurants.name: Jangkeum Tofu & BBQ
restaurants.description: Authentic Korean restaurant specializing in soondubu jjigae (soft tofu stew) and Korean BBQ, reflecting Tenafly's vibrant Korean-American community.

restaurants.name: Classic Cake Company
restaurants.description: Beloved local bakery and cafe known for its cakes, pastries, and light lunch fare. A neighborhood gathering spot.

restaurants.name: Orangetheory Taco (Tenafly Taco)
restaurants.description: Casual Mexican eatery serving fresh tacos, burritos, and guacamole in a cheerful setting on Washington Street.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Alpine Section
parks.description: Spectacular parkland along the top and base of the New Jersey Palisades cliffs, offering hiking trails with stunning views of the Hudson River and the George Washington Bridge. The Long Path and Shore Trail are popular routes.

parks.name: Davis Johnson Park & Gardens
parks.description: A 6-acre borough park featuring beautifully maintained gardens, a gazebo, playground, and walking paths. Popular for community events and quiet relaxation.

parks.name: Roosevelt Common
parks.description: Central green space and recreation area near the municipal complex, featuring athletic fields, a track, and open space for community gatherings.

parks.name: Tenafly Nature Center
parks.description: A 55-acre nature preserve with hiking trails, educational programs, and a visitor center. Offers year-round nature programs for children and adults.

events.name: Tenafly Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Washington Street featuring local vendors, food, live music, and family entertainment. A beloved community tradition.

events.name: Tenafly 5K Road Race
events.description: Annual community road race through the borough's scenic streets, drawing runners of all ages and abilities.

events.name: Concert in the Park Series
events.description: Free summer concert series held in Davis Johnson Park, featuring a variety of musical acts for the whole family.

events.name: Tenafly Nature Center Events
events.description: Year-round nature programs including guided hikes, birding walks, children's camps, and holiday events at the Tenafly Nature Center.

attractions.name: Tenafly Nature Center
attractions.description: A 55-acre nature preserve with marked trails, educational programs, and a visitor center providing an oasis of nature within a suburban setting.

attractions.name: Clinton Inn (Historic)
attractions.description: Historic inn and event venue on Washington Street, a longstanding landmark in Tenafly's downtown.

attractions.name: Palisades Cliffs Scenic Overlooks
attractions.description: Breathtaking views of the Hudson River and Manhattan skyline from various lookout points along the Palisades Interstate Park adjacent to Tenafly.

government.meetingSchedule: Regular meetings are held on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Tenafly Borough Hall, 100 Riveredge Rd, Tenafly, NJ 07670
